business.headline: CRED built a premium user base. Is that why Meta is paying $900 million?Meta is set to invest $900 million in CRED for a reported 20% stake. The deal points to the value of CRED's affluent users as much as its fintech stack.

How Noida's new airport redefines Uttar Pradesh's economic geographyThe start of flights at Noida International Airport marks a new phase for Jewar in UP. Its cargo-led, multi-modal design could reshape exports, industry and regional connectivity across North India.

Did India's compliance push come at the cost of innovation?India has improved ease of doing business over the past decade, but is that enough in a world increasingly shaped by AI, semiconductors and deep technology?

RBI not preparing for interest rate hikes yet, Governor Malhotra clarifiesMalhotra said the RBI is keeping an eye on whether higher oil prices start affecting the prices of other goods and services across the economy. According to him, such broader inflationary pressure has not appeared so far.

Sensex jumps 900 points: Why is stock market rising today?Stock markets rebounded sharply on Wednesday as banking and IT shares led gains after the previous session's sell-off. Lower crude prices and supportive RBI signals lifted sentiment, even as monsoon and US rate risks remained.

Adani AGM: India's future cannot wait, says Gautam Adani on growth roadmapSpeaking at the Adani AGM, Chairman Gautam Adani said the group remained focused on nation-building despite facing challenges over the past year.

IRFC shares fall 5% today. Here's why the railway PSU stock is under pressureIRFC share price: The stock was trading at Rs 93.46 around 9:49 am, down over 5% for the day. The decline adds to the pressure on the PSU stock, which has fallen nearly 7% over the past month, 23% in six months and more than 32% over the past year.

Markets open flat as crude slump offsets hawkish Fed concernsBenchmark indices opened cautiously on Wednesday, with lower crude prices and a rebound in IT stocks aiding stability after the previous session's sell-off. Investors remained watchful of the monsoon, foreign flows and the US Federal Reserve as mixed cues kept sentiment restrained.

8th Pay Commission: OPS demand pending, but govt gives relief to these employeesThe government has said compassionate appointment cases filed by December 31, 2003, can be considered under the old pension rules even if the appointment came later. The clarification offers relief to affected employees while the wider OPS debate continues before the 8th Pay Commission.

Crude oil hits 4-month low. Will petrol and diesel prices fall?Crude oil prices fell to a near four-month low as Middle East tensions eased and supply prospects improved. The drop may help India's import bill, but petrol and diesel prices are unlikely to fall immediately.

Why many Indians earning Rs 1 lakh still struggle to saveFor many Indians, earning Rs 1 lakh a month once symbolised financial success. Yet, despite reaching this milestone, many professionals find themselves living pay cheque to pay cheque, with little left for savings. So where does all the money go?

Sebi looks to widen direct trading access; retail investors could benefitSebi has proposed allowing all categories of investors, including retail participants, to use Direct Market Access (DMA), a facility that enables orders to be placed directly into a stock exchange's trading system through a broker's infrastructure.

Sensex ends 893 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; Infosys falls 3%The S&P BSE Sensex dropped 893.39 points, or 1.16%, to close at 76,200.68, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 278.80 points, or 1.16%, to settle at 23,824.10.

Sensex drops over 900 points: Why is stock market falling today?Dalal Street fell sharply on Tuesday as an Asian market selloff and weak IT shares dragged benchmarks lower. The decline has shifted focus to US rate signals, monsoon worries and fading momentum after the recent rally.

Got your Form 16? Here's why Form 130 won't affect your ITR filing this yearConfused about Form 16 and Form 130? If you're preparing to file your ITR, here's why the upcoming change won't affect your return this year.

Iran wants to sell oil to India after US waiver. Tankers are nearby, but will India buy?Iran has begun pitching crude oil to Indian and other Asian refiners after the US waived sanctions for 60 days amid peace-deal negotiations. With Iranian tankers close to Indian shores, New Delhi appears to be an attractive market for Tehran, but will Indian refiners return to buying Iranian oil?

Pension complaint? Here are 5 ways PFRDA's new AI-powered platform can help youThe Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority has launched a new AI-powered platform called PFRDA Pension Sahayak, aimed at making pension grievance redressal faster, simpler and more accessible.

Seoul's Kospi crashes 10%, Japan's Nikkei falls 4%. Why are Asian markets down?Asian markets tumbled as investors sold tech and AI stocks, with South Korea's Kospi plunging 10% and Japan's Nikkei falling 3.6%.

Expecting an ITR deadline extension? Here's why it may not happen this yearMany taxpayers are hoping for another extension to the ITR filing deadline, as seen in previous years. However, this year's filing season appears different, and the chances of additional time may be lower than expected.

India-US trade talks enter final stretch ahead of July 24 deadlineUnion Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer are meeting in New Delhi to finalise the first phase of the India-US trade pact. The talks are significant as both sides seek tariff gains before the July 24 deadline.

entertainment.headline: Trans actor Trinetra calls Orry's gender-neutral bathroom row divisive, transphobic Trans actor Trinetra criticised Orry's remarks on gender-neutral bathrooms in Kavya Karnatac's podcast and accused the discussion of fear-mongering. Her response has widened the viral debate over women's safety, transgender access and who gets to frame the issue online.

Mouni Roy discusses forgiveness and healing after separation with Suraj Nambiar Mouni Roy has spoken about forgiveness, hurt and spiritual healing after her separation. Her remarks outlined how meditation and letting go have reshaped her response to pain.

Karthi's Sardar 2 release date announced, clashes with Rashmika-Vijay's Ranabaali Karthi's Sardar 2 will release in theatres this September, the makers announced with a new glimpse. The sequel enters a crowded window, opening after Toxic and a day before Ranabaali.

All about musician Fred Again's India tour: Date, time, tickets and what to expect Grammy-winning musician Fred Again is finally bringing his live show to India. Find out the concert dates, ticket details, venues and why his debut tour is generating so much excitement.

Listen responsibly: Chiranjeevi loses his calm, schools fans at Peddi success event Chiranjeevi paused his speech at Peddi's success event to ask fans to listen patiently as he praised Ram Charan. The moment stood out in an otherwise celebratory evening marked by applause, box office cheer and a playful father-son exchange.

Rajinikanth loses patience with trolls: Problem if I talk, problem if I am silent Superstar Rajinikanth, at the launch of Dharman, said that he feels hesitant to now talk in public, claiming that his speeches and his silence attract criticism. The remarks underlined the constant scrutiny around his public appearances and statements.

Mother-to-be Samantha glows at Rahul Ravindran's birthday bash with husband Raj Samantha Ruth Prabhu and Raj Nidimoru attended director Rahul Ravindran's birthday gathering in Hyderabad. The viral pictures also renewed focus on Samantha's pregnancy and her long friendship with Rahul and Chinmayi.

Bhabiji row: Shilpa Shinde labels women who didn't assist her as 'nonsense' Actor Shilpa Shinde called the women who criticised her during the Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain! controversy 'nonsense'. She said they failed to support her when she needed help.

Samay Raina's India's Got Latent 2: Will mainstream caution drown maverick chaos? Samay Raina seemed cautious in Episode 1 of India's Got Latent 2. Fans hope he remembers his promo promise of "no filter". Has the comedian turned wary?

Wanted Re 1 more than Akshay Kumar: Sanjeev Kapoor on rejecting MasterChef India Sanjeev Kapoor said he first turned down MasterChef India after insisting on being paid Re 1 more than Akshay Kumar. He said he later joined in the third season because the show's success mattered to India's wider food entertainment business.

Desi Bling's Tabinda calls husband khuda, shrugs off backlash over his foot massage Tabinda Sanpal has defended the Desi Bling scene showing her massaging husband Satish Sanpal's feet. Her explanation and "majazi khuda" remark have fed a wider debate on gender roles and marriage.

Rajinikanth is a deadly doctor in Ashwath Marmuthu's Dharman, Kamal to produce Rajinikanth's 173rd film has been titled Dharman, with its title and first-look poster unveiled on Wednesday. The project brings together Raaj Kamal Films International and director Ashwath Marimuthu.

How film writers keep failing Samantha's incredible versatility Samantha Ruth Prabhu shines brightly in Maa Inti Bangaram, effortlessly owning action sequences and proving her mettle as both heroine and hero. However, despite her compelling performance, the film's weak screenplay fails to fully support her, highlighting the ongoing need for scripts that truly match her evolving talent.

Did Samay Raina earn Rs 15-20 crore from Netflix for Latent 2? Viral post says so A viral post estimating Samay Raina's earnings from India's Got Latent 2 set off a wide online debate. The discussion soon widened into a closer look at how he could be one of the highest-paid comedians in the industry.

Satish Shah gets Padma Shri posthumously, cousin Arvind Mamania accepts honour President Droupadi Murmu posthumously conferred the Padma Shri on actor Satish Shah, with his cousin Arvind Mamania accepting it on his behalf. The honour prompted an emotional response from his family and colleagues, who said they wished he had received it himself.

Alka Yagnik's update on 'difficult health' after hearing loss: Making my way back Singer Alka Yagnik shared a health update after receiving the Padma Bhushan from President Droupadi Murmu. Her note said she is slowly finding her way back as she continues battling sensorineural nerve hearing loss following a viral attack.

Cocktail 2 vs Cocktail: Sicily, style and Shahid but where's the punch? Cocktail 2 may be creating waves in theatres, but despite its glossy visuals, picturesque locations and star-studded cast, it struggles to recreate the emotional depth and character-driven storytelling that made Cocktail such a memorable watch.

Akshay Kumar reveals his biggest career regret, says he wanted to slap himself Actor Akshay Kumar said he felt like slapping himself after spending a decade doing only action roles. The reflection framed his 35-year career as one of constant reinvention as Welcome to the Jungle nears release.

Why are the women of Toxic fading from the conversation as Yash takes centre stage? With Yash and Geetu Mohandas's Toxic now set for an August 26 release, promotional material increasingly revolves around Raya and Ticket. Is the absence of women deliberate?

general news.headline: Flooded roads, uprooted trees, wall collapse: Monsoon hits Mumbai hard Live UpdatesHeavy rain left key parts of Mumbai, including Dadar station, waterlogged after several areas of the city recorded more than 300 mm of rainfall over the past 24 hours. The rainfall falls in the IMD's "extremely heavy" category, highlighting the intensity of the monsoon downpour.

Hoodie in June heat, phone left at office: How cops cracked Pune fort murderA CCTV camera installed near the fort's ticket counter captured a hooded man, later identified as Chetan Chaudhary, on June 18 trailing Pune businessman Ketan Agarwal and his fiancée Siya Goyal. Just hours later, Ketan was dead.

They won't pay: When Trump laughed off JD Vance's Indian troops in Ukraine planA new book claims Donald Trump rejected JD Vance's proposal to consider Indian peacekeepers for a Ukraine ceasefire mission, saying "Indians won't pay." The account describes internal debates within Trump's administration over ending the Russia-Ukraine war and alternative peacekeeping arrangements.

Bill Gates reveals 3 affairs, blackmail fears in bombshell Epstein testimonyUS lawmakers sought Bill Gates' testimony after the release of additional documents by the Justice Department renewed scrutiny over his past interactions with the disgraced financier, who died in a jail cell in 2019.

Real alien s**t: What are Iran's 'jellyfish' drones, likely behind US jet crash?The pilot of the US F-15E Strike Eagle has said a jellyfish-like drone swarm possibly downed the fighter jet during the Iran conflict. If confirmed, it would mark a major shift in Iran's drone warfare capabilities.

Man sends woman's private pics to her fiance. She, her parents die by suicideRakshita, 21, was set to be married on June 24. However, the accused, Ullas Gowda, sent some photographs and messages of her to her fiance days before the wedding. This led to a confrontation with the groom's family, following which she and her parents consumed poison at home due to extreme distress.

BJP in, TMC out, Kolkata to join Delhi NCR, Mumbai in race for airportsThe BJP-led government in West Bengal has announced plans for a greenfield airport near Kalyani, around 50 km north of Kolkata. Once operational, it will make Kolkata India's third metropolitan region with multiple commercial airports. Mamata Banerjee, as the CM, had resisted the Centre's plan for a second airport.

UK court orders Nirav Modi to repay Rs 100 crore to Bank of India in loan recoveryThe London Circuit Commercial Court ruled that Nirav Modi must personally repay Bank of India under a guarantee he signed for a loan given to Firestar Diamond FZE.

Did India's compliance push come at the cost of innovation?India has improved ease of doing business over the past decade, but is that enough in a world increasingly shaped by AI, semiconductors and deep technology?

Meta says cold email led to Kunal Shah's WhatsApp CEO roleMeta has revealed that Kunal Shah's appointment as WhatsApp CEO began with a cold email from Meta executive Chris Cox. What started as a request for advice soon turned into a leadership offer.

Pune fort murder plan began weeks earlier with stolen passport, cancelled Bali tripPolice allege Siya Goyal stole and misplaced fiance Ketan Agrawal's passport before a Bali trip. Investigators now see the incident as an early step in the murder conspiracy case.

Nihangs clash with modern society with love for weapons, war and cannabisA handful of Nihang Sikhs captured a Rudraprayag gurdwara and engaged in a stand-off with the Uttarakhand administration. The blue-robed Nihangs are associated with war, weapons, horses, cannabis, and controversies. What is their history, and who controls them? It's all explained here.

Mother-to-be Samantha glows at Rahul Ravindran's birthday bash with husband RajSamantha Ruth Prabhu and Raj Nidimoru attended director Rahul Ravindran's birthday gathering in Hyderabad. The viral pictures also renewed focus on Samantha's pregnancy and her long friendship with Rahul and Chinmayi.

Grave sin: BJP shreds Bhagwant Mann amid 'sacrilege' video row; AAP pushes backA viral video allegedly showing a man resembling Bhagwant Mann in objectionable conduct involving Sikh Guru photographs triggered outrage, prompting Akal Takht to declare him "Guru Dokhi" and "Khalsa Panth virodhi."

Whipped, branded, guarded by pitbulls: 13 workers held captive at UP factoryA joint team of labour officials, police and the administration rescued 13 workers from a Muzaffarnagar tote-making factory after an escapee alerted authorities. The case has triggered a probe into alleged bonded labour, assault, illegal confinement and reported deaths in captivity.

Monsoon to sweep across west coast, Maharashtra and northeast todayThe IMD has forecast an active monsoon and rain across several regions, while some parts will continue to battle rising temperatures as monsoon remains active today.

This Indian startup is betting its own money on orbit, twice over. Deets insideA Hyderabad space startup will fly a largely self-funded imaging satellite in the last quarter of 2026, backing its own technology rather than waiting for a customer.

Delhi BJP MLA Kailash Gahlot's daughter Devina tops CUET UG 2026, secures AIR 1Securing AIR 1 in CUET UG 2026, Delhi's former minister Kailash Gahlot's daughter said regular revision, conceptual clarity from NCERT and self-belief were central to her preparation strategy.

Meaningless: Trump lashes out at Senate vote to end war, slams 4 Republican 'losers'US President Donald Trump criticised the Senate after it passed a resolution seeking to limit further military action against Iran without congressional approval. The vote exposed Republican divisions and raised questions over Washington's leverage as talks with Tehran continue.

Pune murder: 2 attempts, fiancée's birthday picnic ruse, and a mournful Insta postA cancelled Bali trip, an alleged failed murder attempt, a birthday picnic at a historic fort and a grieving Instagram post. Investigators say each piece formed part of a chilling plot that ended in death.

Is your resume as strong as Sushila Didi's? This househelp's CV is a career lessonSushila Didi, a househelp's resume, is not your usual CV, and it has gone viral for all the right reasons. The resume is fun, clear and concise. It says much more about how clarity, humour and results can make a CV memorable.

Delhi man finds househelp's 'resume' inside Blinkit order, discovers clever twistA Delhi man found a resume inside his Blinkit order and opened it thinking it was a job application. It turned out to be Urban Company's InstaHelp campaign, which sparked curiosity and online praise.

Cristiano Ronaldo, scrutiny's favourite child, answers with grace and a braceCristiano Ronaldo has spent 23 years turning doubt into fuel, and Houston offered another chapter in that story. Written off after DR Congo, the Portugal captain responded with a brace, a record and a reminder that criticism remains his favourite opponent.

40 drown in France trying to cool off as Europe bakes under record heatA severe heatwave sweeping across Europe has turned deadly, with France reporting 40 drowning deaths in just five days as people sought relief from soaring temperatures. Record heat has disrupted transport, forced school closures and shut major tourist sites, including the Eiffel Tower.

sports.headline: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reminds Kapil Dev of 16-year-old Sachin Tendulkar India's first World Cup-winning captain, Kapil Dev has compared Vaibhav Sooryavanshi to a young Sachin Tendulkar, but said it is far too early to judge the teenager's career.

Axed from Asian Games, Manika Batra threatens to sue table tennis association One of India's top table tennis players, Manika Batra, has threatened legal action against the Table Tennis Federation of India (TTFI). Batra, who was left out of India's Asian Games squad, has demanded transparency from the federation over the selection process.

Stokes is a good friend: McCullum dismisses talk of fallout with England captain England coach Brendon McCullum dismissed speculation of a fallout with captain Ben Stokes, insisting the pair remain "good friends" and have "no idea" where the rumours originated.

Ghana witch doctor strikes? Harry Kane fails to score in England's World Cup draw Ghanaian fans have been celebrating the role of self-styled witch doctor Nana Kwaku Bonsam after Harry Kane endured a frustrating evening in England's goalless draw against Ghana at the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Congo superfan finally arrives in USA, aura farms at FIFA World Cup DR Congo super fans Michel Kuka Mboladinga finally arrived at the FIFA World Cup 2026 on Wednesday, June 24. Standing tall in the team's match against Colombia, Mboladinga was a source of inspiration for the team.

Daniel Munoz sends Colombia into knockouts with narrow win over DR Congo Daniel Munoz's second-half goal finally broke DR Congo's resistance as Colombia secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory in Guadalajara on Tuesday to book a place in the knockout stage of the FIFA World Cup 2026.

Jeremy Doku returns to Belgium squad after son's birth Jeremy Doku is set to rejoin Belgium after leaving camp for the birth of his son. His absence sparked debate, but teammates backed him before a crucial match against New Zealand.

Neymar fit and available but not certain to play vs Scotland, says Ancelotti Neymar is available for Brazil's final Group C clash against Scotland at the FIFA World Cup, but coach Carlo Ancelotti remained tight-lipped on whether the forward will feature in Miami after recovering from a calf injury.

Cristiano Ronaldo, scrutiny's favourite child, answers with grace and a brace Cristiano Ronaldo has spent 23 years turning doubt into fuel, and Houston offered another chapter in that story. Written off after DR Congo, the Portugal captain responded with a brace, a record and a reminder that criticism remains his favourite opponent.

Brave Panama's World Cup fight ends as Croatia stays alive with 1-0 win Ante Budimir's second-half header gave Croatia a 1-0 win over Panama in Toronto. The result kept Croatia alive in Group L and eliminated Panama from the FIFA World Cup 2026.

I don't deserve it: Jude Bellingham's blunt take on MOTM award after Ghana draw Jude Bellingham admitted he did not deserve the Man of the Match award after England's attacking struggles were exposed in a frustrating 0-0 draw against Ghana in Group L in Boston.

All-round Perry gets Australia ready for India tie with Pakistan hammeringEllyse Perry's 71 and 2/9 drove Australia to a 113-run win over Pakistan in Leeds. The result left Australia on the verge of the semi-finals before a high-stakes meeting with India at Lord's.

Second game syndrome hits England hard in lacklustre draw against GhanaEngland were held to a goalless draw by Ghana in Boston after failing to break down a deep defence. The result revived a familiar second-match problem for the Three Lions and raised questions on Thomas Tuchel's tactics in the contest.

France coach Didier Deschamps to miss Norway clash after mother's deathFIFA World Cup 2026: France coach Didier Deschamps has returned home after the death of his mother and will miss Friday's World Cup match against Norway. Assistant Guy Stephan will take charge as France, already through, look to finish top of Group I.

Iran catch a break: US relaxes World Cup travel rules before Egypt clashFIFA World Cup 2026: The United States has allowed Iran to enter Seattle two days before its World Cup match against Egypt. The change gives Team Melli more preparation time, though post-match exit rules remain in place.

Cristiano Ronaldo ignores reporter's Messi question after Portugal brace. VideoFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo's record-breaking night for Portugal took an unexpected turn when he appeared to ignore a question about Lionel Messi. The brief interaction quickly went viral, reigniting discussion around football's most iconic rivalry.

3 injured in stampede outside Ranchi stadium during Jharkhand T20 LeagueA crowd surge at the West Gate before the Jharkhand T20 League final left spectators injured at Ranchi's JSCA Stadium on Tuesday, June 23.

Written off again, Cristiano Ronaldo answers with a brace as Portugal hit fiveFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo arrived in Houston surrounded by questions about age, relevance and whether Portugal had outgrown him. Ninety minutes later, he left with a brace, another World Cup record and a reminder that doubting him remains football's favourite mistake.

COL 1-0 COD highlights: Colombia beat DR Congo to reach knockoutsColombia (COL) vs DR Congo (COD) highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Colombia booked their place in the knockout stage of the 2026 World Cup with a hard-fought 1-0 win over DR Congo in Guadalajara. The deadlock was finally broken in the 76th minute when Daniel Munoz found the net, ending the resistance of goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi, who had produced a series of fine saves to keep the Africans in the contest.

Cristiano Ronaldo silences critics, becomes 1st player to score in 6 World CupsCristiano Ronaldo scored twice against Uzbekistan in Houston as Portugal took control. His opener made him the first player to score in six World Cups and answered criticism after the draw with DR Congo.

3,784 days of dominance: India beat Pakistan in 7-goal London hockey thrillerIndia edged Pakistan 4-3 in a chaotic FIH Pro League clash in London. The win stretched India's unbeaten run against Pakistan to 3,784 days despite a frantic late fightback.

Tejaswin Shankar gets AFI exemption, stays in Asian Games selection raceAFI has granted Tejaswin Shankar exemption from the National Inter-State Championships and will still consider him for the Asian Games. The decision acknowledges the recovery demands of the decathlon and keeps the national record holder in contention.

POR vs UZB Highlights: Undeniable Ronaldo, dominant Portugal hit 5 in routPortugal (POR) vs Uzbekistan (UZB) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo turned the spotlight into a stage, scoring twice as Portugal dismantled Uzbekistan in Houston. With Bruno Fernandes pulling the strings and set-pieces doing the damage, Roberto Martinez's side have looked every bit the contenders they promised to be.

New Zealand survive Scotland scare, remain alive in semis raceWomen's T20 Wolrd CUp 2026: Izzy Sharp and Brooke Halliday revived New Zealand to chase 132 after an early wobble. Darcey Carter's unbeaten 72 proved insufficient as Scotland's semi-final hopes ended, keeping New Zealand alive.

IPL trade buzz: KKR, Rajasthan open talks with MI over potential Hardik Pandya moveKolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals have approached Mumbai Indians over a potential Hardik Pandya trade. Rajasthan are eyeing a swap deal involving opening batter Yashasvi Jaiswal.

IND vs PAK hockey highlights: India beat Pakistan in 7-goal thrillerIndia (IND) vs Pakistan (PAK) hockey highlights, FIH Pro League: Pakistan bounced back with two late goals, but it was not enough as India won the seven-goal thriller 4-3 in London. Goals from Abhishek, Nilakanta Sharma, Sukhjeet Singh and Rajinder Singh sealed the win for India.

Rohit Sharma receives Padma Shri from President Murmu, wife Ritika beams with prideIndian cricketer Rohit Sharma, hockey star Savita Punia and tennis great Vijay Amritraj were among the sports stars who received their Padma Awards from President Droupadi Murmu on Tuesday in New Delhi.

Jordan knocked out of FIFA World Cup as resilient Algeria stage comeback victoryFIFA World Cup: Jordan became the first team from Group J to be knocked out of the FIFA World Cup. Despite taking the lead in the first half, Jordan let it slip in two crucial moments and ended up losing 2-1 to Algeria.

Sree Charani becomes No. 1 T20I bowler a year after making international debutSree Charani became the world's top-ranked T20I bowler just over a year after her India debut. The left-arm spinner has been in fine form in the ongoing Women's T20 World Cup campaign in England.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
